summary:
The patient’s symptoms are most likely due to diabetic peripheral neuropathy secondary to long-standing, inadequately controlled Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus. Optimal management requires aggressive improvement in glycaemic control as a primary intervention to prevent further neurological deterioration and limb-threatening complications such as ulceration and ischemia.
